Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed verbal clause (adverbial + progressive verb form)
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To act foolishly or behave without reasoning in a situation requiring judgment.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in agrarian metaphors of wandering livestock in Kokrajhar villages.
First Example Sentence: Nwng gwjwnai gwdan, sikhlao mwnnai nai.
Third Example Sentence: Angni fisa gwjwnai gwdan jwngnai mwjang nai.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Parallel compound clause with adjectival predicates
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Good days reflect a good future; prosperity follows preparation.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Associated with Bwisagu agricultural optimism and renewal symbolism.

Third Example Sentence: Angni swrwngthai phwrwi dwisa mwjang jwngni jwla mwjang.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To ignore someone deliberately despite knowing the truth.

Third Example Sentence: Jwngha rajnitini khonthai nai mwnnai khou jwngnai.
